track: Track B of post-multifocal-catalogue parallel sprint

headline_verdict.sprint_scale_first_deliverable: GO on Candidate A (Wick-rotation-map memo for Sprint TD Track 4 with Bisognano-Wichmann reading)
headline_verdict.rationale_one_sentence: Lorentzian propinquity has no published prescription as of May 2026, making the dominant cost (6-12 months original NCG-math work) HIGH-risk; the sprint-scale alternative captures the strategically valuable Bisognano-Wichmann interpretation of existing thermal results at 1-week cost.

verified_citations.ref: Strohmaier, A. 'On noncommutative and pseudo-Riemannian geometry.' J. Geom. Phys. 56, 175-195 (2006). arXiv:math-ph/0110001.
verified_citations.structural_ingredient: Foundational pseudo-Riemannian spectral triple definition. Hilbert -> Krein space, fundamental symmetry J^2 = I, Krein-self-adjoint Dirac.
verified_citations.geovac_notes: Camporesi-Higuchi Dirac on S^3 is currently Krein-self-adjoint trivially with J = I. Lorentzian extension would need non-trivial J picking out temporal direction.

verified_citations.ref: Paschke, M. and Verch, R. 'Local covariant quantum field theory over spectral geometries.' Class. Quantum Grav. 21, 5299-5316 (2004). arXiv:gr-qc/0405057.
verified_citations.structural_ingredient: Categorical functor from globally hyperbolic Lorentz manifolds to involutive algebras.
verified_citations.geovac_notes: Categorical scaffolding for gluing GeoVac S^3 slices at different times into globally hyperbolic spectral triple. Not a concrete sprint deliverable.

verified_citations.ref: Bizi, N., Brouder, C., and Besnard, F. 'Space and time dimensions of algebras with applications to Lorentzian noncommutative geometry and quantum electrodynamics.' J. Math. Phys. 59, 062303 (2018). arXiv:1611.07062.
verified_citations.structural_ingredient: (m,n) classification: pair of space/time dimensions mod 8. Tensor product additivity. Indefinite spectral triple over Krein space.
verified_citations.geovac_notes: Current GeoVac at (m,n) = (3,0). Lorentzian extension at (3,1). Tensor T_{S^3} (x) T_{S^1_t} = (3,0) + (0,1) = (3,1). What's missing: propinquity at (m,n) != (k,0).

verified_citations.ref: Bizi, N. 'Semi-Riemannian Noncommutative Geometry, Gauge Theory, and the Standard Model of Particle Physics.' Thesis (2018). arXiv:1812.00038.
verified_citations.structural_ingredient: Extended (m,n) framework. Constructs Lorentzian SM spectral triple.

verified_citations.ref: van den Dungen, K. 'Krein spectral triples and the fermionic action.' Math. Phys. Anal. Geom. 19, 4 (2016). doi:10.1007/s11040-016-9207-z.
verified_citations.structural_ingredient: Krein-space fermionic action <psi, J D psi>.

verified_citations.ref: Franco, N. and Eckstein, M. 'Temporal Lorentzian Spectral Triples.' Reviews in Mathematical Physics 26, 1430007 (2014). arXiv:1210.6575.
verified_citations.structural_ingredient: Temporal Lorentzian triple = pseudo-Riemannian + 3+1 decomposition + global-time element T in A. Lorentzian distance formula d(p,q) = sup (a(q) - a(p)).
verified_citations.geovac_notes: Promoting Sprint TD Track 1's S^3 (x) S^1_beta to a Franco-Eckstein temporal Lorentzian triple is at least 4-6 weeks of careful work.

verified_citations.ref: Connes, A. and Moscovici, H. 'Type III and spectral triples.' In Traces in Number Theory, Geometry and Quantum Fields, Vieweg, pp. 57-71 (2008). arXiv:math/0609703.
verified_citations.structural_ingredient: Twisted spectral triple (A, H, D, sigma) with automorphism sigma.

verified_citations.ref: Devastato, A., Lizzi, F., and Martinetti, P. 'Lorentz signature and twisted spectral triples.' JHEP 03, 089 (2018). arXiv:1710.04965.
verified_citations.structural_ingredient: Twisting SM spectral triple yields Krein space with Lorentzian signature. Twist as alternative to Wick rotation.
verified_citations.geovac_notes: GeoVac outer factor is rank-1; twist would have to act on Camporesi-Higuchi directly, not on inner factor. Not a sprint deliverable.

verified_citations.ref: Nieuviarts, G. 'Torsion and Lorentz symmetry from Twisted Spectral Triples.' arXiv:2401.07848 (2024).
verified_citations.structural_ingredient: Generates orthogonal geodesic-preserving torsion from torsionless Dirac via twist.

verified_citations.ref: Nieuviarts, G. 'Emergence of Lorentz symmetry from an almost-commutative twisted spectral triple.' arXiv:2502.18105 (May 2025).
verified_citations.structural_ingredient: Riemannian twisted -> pseudo-Riemannian spectral triple within almost-commutative structure, no explicit Wick rotation, no complex numbers.

verified_citations.ref: Author(s). 'Emergence of Time from a Twisted Spectral Triple in Almost-Commutative Geometry.' arXiv:2512.15450 (Dec 2025).
verified_citations.structural_ingredient: Time emerges from twisted spectral data within almost-commutative SM framework. Pathway from Euclidean NCG to Lorentzian physics.

verified_citations.ref: Connes, A., Consani, C., and Moscovici, H. 'Zeta Spectral Triples.' arXiv:2511.22755 (Nov 2025).
verified_citations.structural_ingredient: Twisted spectral triples with spectra matching Riemann zeta zeros at high frequency.

verified_citations.ref: Latremoliere, F. 'The dual Gromov-Hausdorff propinquity.' J. Math. Pures Appl. 103, 303-351 (2017). 'The Gromov-Hausdorff propinquity for metric Spectral Triples.' arXiv:1811.10843 (2018).
verified_citations.structural_ingredient: Riemannian propinquity theory used in Paper 38 WH1 PROVEN.

verified_citations.ref: Hekkelman, J. and McDonald, E. 'Spectral truncations of compact quantum metric spaces.' arXiv:2412.00628.
verified_citations.structural_ingredient: Riemannian spectral truncations on flat-tower / S^1 / S^2 / T^d.

verified_citations.ref: Leimbach, F. and van Suijlekom, W. 'Convergence of spectral truncations of the torus.' Adv. Math. 439, 109496 (2024).
verified_citations.structural_ingredient: Riemannian on torus T^d; reference for Paper 38 L2 lemma.

verified_citations.ref: Bisognano, J.J. and Wichmann, E.H. 'On the duality condition for a Hermitian scalar field.' J. Math. Phys. 17, 303 (1976).
verified_citations.structural_ingredient: Modular flow of vacuum state on wedge IS unitary representation of Lorentz boosts. Bridge between Wick-rotated thermal and Lorentz-boost physics.

verified_citations.ref: Sewell, G.L. 'Quantum fields on manifolds: PCT and gravitationally induced thermal states.' Ann. Phys. 141, 201 (1980).
verified_citations.structural_ingredient: Schwarzschild -> KMS at beta = 8 pi M via modular flow.

phantom_or_unverified.note: Closest match in literature is Brouder-Besnard-Bizi 2017 'Krein Spectral Triples and the Fermionic Action' (Math. Phys. Anal. Geom. 19) or van den Dungen 2016 (same title). Recommend removing Hawkins-Skoda from candidate list; possible phantom citation.

literature_picture_summary.implication: Multiple Lorentzian-NCG frameworks exist (pseudo-Riemannian, temporal, twisted-emergence) but NONE provides Lorentzian propinquity. WH1 PROVEN's GH-convergence theorem has no Lorentzian analog in the literature.

blocking_axioms.reason: Quantum compact metric space framework requires Lipschitz seminorm L(a) = ||[D,a]||; Krein-norm ||[D,a]||_J is not positive-definite; no published Lorentzian propinquity
Heat kernel Tr e^(-tD^2) requires elliptic D; Minkowski D^2 hyperbolic; Mellin transform changes character (Schwinger proper-time)
Boost mixes spatial L with linear momentum (Wigner rotations); spatial L not conserved; expansion does not terminate

extending_axioms.axiom: Dirac operator on S^3 x R_t

trivial_axioms: M1 master Mellin (Bisognano-Wichmann gives same 2 pi)
Roothaan termination at v=0 (bare Riemannian remains)

Doesn't address spinor content under boost
candidates.falsifier: None; Bisognano-Wichmann reading is standard physics

candidates.name: SO(4,1) conformal-isometry algebra on Fock-projected S^3
what_it_lands: 10 generators of so(4,1) = conf(S^3) on (n,l,m) basis at finite n_max
Action of 3 boost-like generators: Delta n, Delta l, Delta m transitions
Whether boost generators preserve Roothaan termination
Whether Connes axioms preserved under conformal action
candidates.falsifier: SO(4,1) preserved by truncation in non-trivial way (would be positive surprise; expected outcome is broken at finite n_max because boosts raise n)
candidates.expected_outcome: SO(4,1) broken; spatial SO(4) preserved; conformal-boost intertwiner between truncation levels n_max and n_max + 1 is the new mathematical object

candidates.name: Krein-lift Connes-axiom audit at (m,n) = (3,1)
what_it_lands: New module debug/krein_lift_audit.py extending Paper 32 IV finite-n_max audit
Krein-side stress test of Bizi-Brouder-Besnard sign table at (3,1)
Verify J^2 sign in temporal extension; JD = +/- DJ
candidates.what_it_does_not_close: No propinquity, no convergence, no M2 reformulation
candidates.falsifier: Discrepancy with Bizi-Brouder-Besnard prescription would indicate GeoVac J does not fit standard Lorentzian framework

result_30_second_read.multi_month_go_no_go: NO-GO at this time; cost 9-18 months dominated by 6-12 months original Lorentzian-propinquity NCG-math; strategic value medium; risk of stalling high; opportunity cost vs multi-focal precision arc unfavorable; revisit if Lorentzian propinquity lands in literature or twisted-emergence stabilizes
